author: gb9t
content:
**Phase B advance: kernel/ tier complete (largest subsystem).**

.design/kernel/00-overview.md (465 lines, 19 REQ / 18 AC, 5 open).

Coverage: 4/14 in-scope subsystems with Tier-2 drafts (arch + arch/x86, lib, mm, kernel).

30+ Tier-3 docs spawned (planned, none written) — kernel/ has more sub-overviews than mm/. Top-level Tier-3 areas:
- sched/ (subdir: 00-overview + cfs, rt, deadline, ext, idle, pelt, topology, clock, cpufreq)
- locking/ (subdir: 00-overview + mutex, spinlock, rwsem, seqlock, rcu, lockdep)
- time/ (subdir: 00-overview + hrtimer, clocksource, tick, posix-timers, namespace)
- cgroup/ (subdir: 00-overview + core, cpuset, pids, freezer)
- bpf/ (subdir: 00-overview + verifier, program-types, maps, btf, lsm, iter, helpers, arena, struct-ops)
- trace/ (subdir: 00-overview + ftrace, tracepoints, kprobes, uprobes, blktrace, eventfs)
Plus single-file Tier-3 docs: task-lifecycle, futex, irq, workqueue, smp-hotplug, perf-events, module-loading, printk, power-mgmt, syscall-entry-helpers, dma-mapping, livepatch, kgdb, unwind, coverage, audit, crash-kexec, panic-reboot, sysctl-ksysfs, kallsyms, runtime-codepatching, accounting.

Compat surface owned by kernel/:
- Every process-lifecycle syscall (clone3/fork/exec/exit/wait/setsid/setpgid/prctl/arch_prctl)
- All signal syscalls (rt_sig*, signalfd, pidfd_send_signal, sigaltstack)
- All credential syscalls (set/get uid/gid/groups, capset/capget)
- All scheduler syscalls (sched_setattr/getattr/setaffinity/getaffinity/setscheduler)
- All time syscalls (clock_*, timer_*, timerfd, nanosleep, adjtimex, posix_timers)
- futex(2) full ABI incl. PI-futex
- bpf(2) full ABI + BTF binary format + program/map types
- perf_event_open(2)
- ftrace ABI under /sys/kernel/tracing/
- /proc/<pid>/ task fields (stat/status/sched/wchan/stack/cgroup/ns/...)
- /proc/{interrupts,softirqs,sched_debug,timer_list,kallsyms,locks,modules}
- /sys/kernel/{tracing,btf,livepatch}/*, /sys/devices/system/cpu/{topology,vulnerabilities}
- /sys/fs/{cgroup,bpf}/*
- dmesg / /dev/kmsg format
- panic / oops / BUG output format
- module signing + .ko binary load
- KGDB serial protocol

Verification:
- Layer 1: 8 anticipated unsafe-block clusters
- Layer 2: 13 mandatory TLA+ models — runqueue, load_balance, preempt, qspinlock, qrwlock, rcu_grace_period, percpu_rwsem, futex_wait_wake, futex_pi, cgroup_hierarchy, workqueue_pool, bpf_verifier_soundness, printk_ringbuf, trace_ringbuf
- Layer 3: 5 mandatory invariant harnesses (CFS rbtree ordering + RT priority + DL deadline + PI-graph acyclicity + cgroup accounting) — kernel/sched/ is the third of four mandatory Layer-3 subsystems per D4
- Layer 4 opt-ins: RCU grace-period algorithm (Verus), BPF verifier soundness (Verus, may slip past v0), CFS fairness theorems (Verus/Creusot), DL schedulability (Verus)

5 open questions:
Q1: sched_ext (BPF scheduler) in v0 — recommend YES (distros ship scx tools)
Q2: BPF verifier — re-implement in Rust (high effort, high value, Verus-friendly) or FFI black box (betrays project ambition) — recommend re-implement
Q3: liveupdate — out of scope for v0 (depends on serializing arbitrary kernel state)
Q4: audit subsystem — IS in v0 (CONFIG_AUDIT=y is RHEL/Fedora/SLES default)
Q5: ftrace depth — full ftrace OR minimal tracepoints-only — recommend FULL (existing tools depend on it)

All upstream paths verified to resolve under /home/doll/linux-src.

Continuing dependency order: fs/00-overview.md next.
